hooks.type: core survival / secure leverage against debt and exploitation
hooks.notes: South Third’s truth remains a leverage battlefield; Rowan explicitly notes the fight is costing him time and money he does not have.

hooks.type: Bell Vault / buried structure / family connection mystery
hooks.notes: Rowan finds a probable hidden access point with matching broken bell-resonance and interior airflow; family caution around buried sound is recalled again.

hooks.type: Rowan’s unusual listening trait and its limits
hooks.notes: Listening identifies a dead acoustic pocket and sealed space, but the cost worsens with metallic taste and deepening headache.

hooks.type: institutional suppression / who controls the story
hooks.notes: Maelin holds district control through dawn, Hadrik pushes supplemental capture, and Bren shifts toward off-book maneuvering; packet control remains central.

hooks.type: Benn and Derren: missing-party truth and possible survivors
hooks.notes: Alternate-egress logic strengthens survival possibility; Rowan promises Mara he will bring back any truth about Benn whole.

hooks.type: who sealed/hidden the older structure and why
hooks.notes: Rowan finds patched concealment work “done to disappear,” implying deliberate and skilled suppression of access.

hooks.type: Rowan’s legal exposure over route knowledge / sealed testimony risk
hooks.notes: Hadrik explicitly frames omission of possible egress as dangerous; first-bell supplement increases risk of formal consequences.

hooks.type: Elias/family strain under debt and danger
hooks.notes: Elias remains off-page, but Rowan’s memory of his father’s warning about buried sound deepens the family-history thread.

hooks.type: disturbed markers from inside South Third
hooks.notes: Rowan states publicly that markers may have been disturbed from inside or by someone exiting another way, tying them directly to the hidden route theory.

hooks.type: transferred bell foundation with sealed interior access
hooks.notes: New field evidence in the west service court supports the sealed-interior-access model beyond the street crack itself.
Pell confronts Rowan, accepts partial concealment, grants time until first bell, and offers careful wording for later disclosure.

hooks.type: something still moving or breathing within the sealed structure
hooks.notes: Pulsed air continues at South Third, and the hidden wall also carries airflow from within, reinforcing linked internal space and possible movement.
Maelin successfully holds the night seal against Review and contractor pressure, but only until dawn.

hooks.type: alternate entrance suspicion becomes active race
hooks.notes: Suspicion turns into a concrete lead when Rowan locates a probable route in the old service court.

hooks.type: first-bell deadline before Pell’s supplement locks the record
hooks.notes: Pell gives Rowan only until first bell to produce something usable before the official packet expands.

hooks.type: Mara’s surveillance of Bren’s off-book moves
hooks.notes: Mara is tasked to watch departing contractors, packet movement, split crews, and quiet tools.
